messages.subject: compute_player_form_features() + nrl_player_stats scraper
messages.body:
Builder 1 has created:

1. scraping/nrl_player_stats.py (NEW)
   - fetch_round_player_stats(year, round_num, delay=0.3) -> list[dict]
   - fetch_season_player_stats(year) -> list[dict]
   - backfill_all_player_stats(start_year=2015, end_year=2026) -> pd.DataFrame
   - Saves to: data/processed/player_match_stats.parquet
   - Columns: year, round, match_id, team, player_id, player_name, jersey_number,
              player_position, is_starter, is_spine, + 22 stat fields
              (allRunMetres, lineBreaks, tackleBreaks, tryAssists, fantasyPointsTotal,
               minutesPlayed, errors, handlingErrors, missedTackles, offloads,
               postContactMetres, kicks, kickMetres, penalties, ineffectiveTackles,
               passes, receipts, dummyHalfRuns, allRuns, tacklesMade, lineBreakAssists, tries)

2. pipelines/v4.py — added compute_player_form_features(matches, player_stats_df)
   from pipelines.v4 import compute_player_form_features
   Signature: compute_player_form_features(matches: pd.DataFrame, player_stats_df: pd.DataFrame | None) -> pd.DataFrame
   
   Adds 42 columns to matches:
   - Spine form (4 stats × 2 windows × 3 sides): home/away_spine_run_metres_{3,5}, 
     home/away_spine_line_breaks_{3,5}, home/away_spine_tackle_breaks_{3,5},
     home/away_spine_try_assists_{3,5}, spine_diff_*_{3,5}
   - Squad form (2 stats × 2 windows × 3 sides): home/away_squad_fantasy_{3,5},
     home/away_squad_minutes_{3,5}, squad_diff_*_{3,5}
   - Disruption (6): home/away_spine_changes, spine_changes_diff,
     home/away_squad_turnover, squad_turnover_diff

NOTE for predict_round.py integration (Part C — blocked for me by file ownership):
The caller needs to:
1. In load_historical_data(): load player_match_stats.parquet → player_stats
2. Return player_stats alongside matches, ladders, odds, match_stats
3. In build_features(): add player_stats param, call:
      all_matches = v4.compute_player_form_features(all_matches, player_stats)
4. Add to FEATURE_COLS (near "# V4 Rolling match stats features (60)" comment):
      # V4 Player form features (42) — see pipelines/v4.py constants for full list
      *[f"home_spine_{s}_{w}" for w in [3,5] for s in ["run_metres","line_breaks","tackle_breaks","try_assists"]],
      *[f"away_spine_{s}_{w}" for w in [3,5] for s in ["run_metres","line_breaks","tackle_breaks","try_assists"]],
      *[f"spine_diff_{s}_{w}" for w in [3,5] for s in ["run_metres","line_breaks","tackle_breaks","try_assists"]],
      *[f"home_squad_{s}_{w}" for w in [3,5] for s in ["fantasy","minutes"]],
      *[f"away_squad_{s}_{w}" for w in [3,5] for s in ["fantasy","minutes"]],
      *[f"squad_diff_{s}_{w}" for w in [3,5] for s in ["fantasy","minutes"]],
      "home_spine_changes","away_spine_changes","spine_changes_diff",
      "home_squad_turnover","away_squad_turnover","squad_turnover_diff",
5. Update both build_features() call sites in main() to pass player_stats.

messages.subject: Builder 2 done — refresh_week.py needs manual patch (file guard blocked)
messages.body:
Builder 2 complete. scripts/backtest_player_features.py committed.

BLOCKED: refresh_week.py was not in my assigned files list, so the guard blocked edit.
The step_scrape_player_stats() function needs to be manually added.

EXACT PATCH for refresh_week.py:

1. Insert this function BEFORE def step2_update_matches() (around line 196):

```python
def step_scrape_player_stats(year: int, round_num: int):
    """Scrape per-player match stats from NRL.com API for the just-completed round.

    Fetches individual player stats (tackles, runs, line breaks, etc.) for all
    matches in the round and appends them to player_match_stats.parquet.
    Idempotent: deduplicates on (year, round, match_id, player_id).
    """
    print(f"\n  STEP 1c: Scraping player stats for Round {round_num} of {year}...")

    try:
        from scraping.nrl_match_stats import fetch_round_player_stats
    except ImportError as e:
        print(f"    SKIPPED: could not import fetch_round_player_stats — {e}")
        return

    try:
        new_stats = fetch_round_player_stats(year, round_num)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"    ERROR fetching player stats: {e}")
        return

    if not new_stats:
        print(f"    No player stats returned for Round {round_num} — skipping")
        return

    new_df = pd.DataFrame(new_stats)
    if new_df.empty:
        print(f"    Empty player stats DataFrame — skipping")
        return

    print(f"    Fetched {len(new_df)} player stat rows")

    stats_path = PROCESSED_DIR / "player_match_stats.parquet"
    if stats_path.exists():
        existing = pd.read_parquet(stats_path)
        combined = pd.concat([existing, new_df], ignore_index=True)
        # Deduplicate: keep last occurrence (most recently scraped)
        dedup_cols = [c for c in ["year", "round", "match_id", "player_id"] if c in combined.columns]
        if dedup_cols:
            before = len(combined)
            combined = combined.drop_duplicates(subset=dedup_cols, keep="last").reset_index(drop=True)
            dupes = before - len(combined)
            if dupes > 0:
                print(f"    Removed {dupes} duplicate rows")
    else:
        combined = new_df

    combined.to_parquet(stats_path, index=False, engine="pyarrow")
    print(f"    Saved player_match_stats.parquet — {len(combined)} total rows "
          f"({len(new_df)} new for Round {round_num})")
```

2. In main(), change this block (around line 391):
```python
            new_matches, new_ladders = step1_scrape_round(year, round_num)
            step_scrape_match_stats(year, round_num)
            step2_update_matches(new_matches, year, round_num)
```
TO:
```python
            new_matches, new_ladders = step1_scrape_round(year, round_num)
            step_scrape_match_stats(year, round_num)
            step_scrape_player_stats(year, round_num)
            step2_update_matches(new_matches, year, round_num)
```
